title: Adopt an extracted package back into the project
description: Replace a local library (previously marked by mark-extractable and published as @bespunky/<name>) with the published npm package, closing the extraction loop. Default flow honours verify-then-delete (decision #3): the first run adds the package + rewrites imports but KEEPS the local lib; you build to verify the package works; then re-run with --finalize to remove the now-unused local lib. Use --keepShim to instead leave the lib as a thin re-export of the package (staged migration).

lib.description: The local library project to adopt away (replace with the published package).
lib.x-prompt: Which local library is being replaced by the published package?

package.description: Published package name (e.g. @bespunky/<name>). Defaults to the lib's extraction.json ingestedPackage/proposedPackage.

finalize.description: Step 2: remove the now-unused local library. Run this only AFTER building to verify the published package works.

keepShim.description: Instead of rewrite + delete, replace the local library's entry with `export * from '<package>'` and keep it (staged migration). One step, no finalize.
